#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
cd "$SCRIPT_DIR"

RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
NC='\033[0m'

FAILED=0

log_pass() { echo -e "${GREEN}[PASS]${NC} $1"; }
log_fail() { echo -e "${RED}[FAIL]${NC} $1"; FAILED=1; }
log_info() { echo -e "${YELLOW}[INFO]${NC} $1"; }

# Port configuration (must match docker-compose.yml)
API_PORT=8081
UI_PORT=3001

# ============================================================
# Phase 1: Docker build and startup
# ============================================================
log_info "Phase 1: Building and starting Docker containers..."

docker compose down -v --remove-orphans 2>/dev/null || true

if docker compose build --no-cache 2>&1; then
    log_pass "Docker build succeeded"
else
    log_fail "Docker build failed"
    exit 1
fi

docker compose up -d

# Wait for backend to be healthy
log_info "Waiting for services to become healthy..."
RETRIES=30
HEALTHY=0
for i in $(seq 1 $RETRIES); do
    if curl -sf http://localhost:${API_PORT}/api/health >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        HEALTHY=1
        break
    fi
    sleep 2
done

if [ "$HEALTHY" -eq 1 ]; then
    log_pass "All services are healthy"
else
    log_fail "Services did not become healthy in time"
    docker compose logs
    docker compose down -v
    exit 1
fi

# ============================================================
# Phase 2: Health check endpoints
# ============================================================
log_info "Phase 2: Verifying endpoints..."

if curl -sf http://localhost:${API_PORT}/api/health | grep -q '"status":"ok"'; then
    log_pass "Backend health check returned OK"
else
    log_fail "Backend health check failed"
fi

if curl -sf http://localhost:${UI_PORT}/ | grep -q 'WLPR Portal'; then
    log_pass "Frontend serves HTML with app title"
else
    log_fail "Frontend did not return expected HTML"
fi

if curl -sf http://localhost:${UI_PORT}/config.js | grep -q '__WLPR_CONFIG__'; then
    log_pass "Runtime config.js is served correctly"
else
    log_fail "Runtime config.js not found or invalid"
fi

# ============================================================
# Phase 3: Registration & API smoke tests
# ============================================================
log_info "Phase 3: Registration and API smoke tests..."

# Register admin user (role selected at registration)
REG_RESP=$(curl -sf -X POST http://localhost:${API_PORT}/api/auth/register \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '{"username":"testadmin","email":"testadmin@test.local","password":"TestAdmin@2024!","display_name":"Test Administrator","role":"system_admin"}')

if echo "$REG_RESP" | grep -q 'system_admin\|system admin'; then
    log_pass "Admin user registered with system_admin role"
else
    log_fail "Admin registration failed: $REG_RESP"
fi

# Login as the registered admin
LOGIN_RESP=$(curl -sf -X POST http://localhost:${API_PORT}/api/auth/login \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '{"username":"testadmin","password":"TestAdmin@2024!"}')

if echo "$LOGIN_RESP" | grep -q '"token"'; then
    log_pass "Admin login succeeded"
    TOKEN=$(echo "$LOGIN_RESP" | grep -o '"token":"[^"]*"' | head -1 | cut -d'"' -f4)
else
    log_fail "Admin login failed: $LOGIN_RESP"
    TOKEN=""
fi

if [ -n "$TOKEN" ]; then
    # Test authenticated endpoint
    if curl -sf -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" http://localhost:${API_PORT}/api/auth/me | grep -q '"user_id"'; then
        log_pass "GET /api/auth/me returns user data"
    else
        log_fail "GET /api/auth/me failed"
    fi

    # Test search (empty results expected — no seeded content)
    if curl -sf -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" "http://localhost:${API_PORT}/api/search?q=test" | grep -q '"results"'; then
        log_pass "GET /api/search returns valid response"
    else
        log_fail "GET /api/search failed"
    fi

    # Test learning paths (empty expected — no seeded data)
    PATHS_RESP=$(curl -sf -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" http://localhost:${API_PORT}/api/learning/paths)
    if [ "$PATHS_RESP" = "null" ] || echo "$PATHS_RESP" | grep -q '\[\]'; then
        log_pass "GET /api/learning/paths returns empty (no seeded data)"
    else
        log_pass "GET /api/learning/paths returns valid response"
    fi

    # Register a second user with learner role
    REG2_RESP=$(curl -sf -X POST http://localhost:${API_PORT}/api/auth/register \
        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
        -d '{"username":"testlearner","email":"learner@test.local","password":"Learner@2024!","display_name":"Test Learner","role":"learner"}')

    if echo "$REG2_RESP" | grep -q 'learner'; then
        log_pass "Second user registered with learner role"
    else
        log_fail "Second user registration response unexpected: $REG2_RESP"
    fi

    # Login as learner and verify RBAC invisibility (404 not 403)
    LEARNER_RESP=$(curl -sf -X POST http://localhost:${API_PORT}/api/auth/login \
        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
        -d '{"username":"testlearner","password":"Learner@2024!"}')
    LEARNER_TOKEN=$(echo "$LEARNER_RESP" | grep -o '"token":"[^"]*"' | head -1 | cut -d'"' -f4)

    if [ -n "$LEARNER_TOKEN" ]; then
        HTTP_CODE=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
            -H "Authorization: Bearer $LEARNER_TOKEN" \
            http://localhost:${API_PORT}/api/procurement/settlements)
        if [ "$HTTP_CODE" = "404" ]; then
            log_pass "RBAC: Learner gets 404 for /settlements (feature invisible)"
        else
            log_fail "RBAC: Learner got $HTTP_CODE instead of 404 for /settlements"
        fi

        # Learner should be able to access search
        HTTP_CODE2=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
            -H "Authorization: Bearer $LEARNER_TOKEN" \
            "http://localhost:${API_PORT}/api/search?q=test")
        if [ "$HTTP_CODE2" = "200" ]; then
            log_pass "Learner can access /search (200)"
        else
            log_fail "Learner got $HTTP_CODE2 for /search"
        fi
    fi
fi

# ============================================================
# Phase 4: Integration tests against running DB
# ============================================================
log_info "Phase 4: Running integration tests against containerized DB..."

NETWORK_NAME=$(docker network ls --format '{{.Name}}' | grep repo | head -1)
if [ -z "$NETWORK_NAME" ]; then
    NETWORK_NAME="repo_default"
fi

# Copy backend to a temp volume so go mod tidy can write go.sum without polluting the host
if docker run --rm --network="$NETWORK_NAME" \
    -e TEST_DATABASE_URL="postgres://wlpr:wlpr_secret@db:5432/wlpr_portal?sslmode=disable" \
    -v "$SCRIPT_DIR/backend:/src:ro" \
    -v "$SCRIPT_DIR/config/config.js:/config/config.js:ro" \
    golang:1.22-alpine sh -c "
        cp -r /src /app && cd /app && go mod tidy &&
        go test -v -tags=integration -count=1 -timeout=120s ./internal/repository/ 2>&1
    " ; then
    log_pass "Integration tests passed"
else
    log_fail "Integration tests failed"
fi

# ============================================================
# Phase 5: Unit tests (no DB required)
# ============================================================
log_info "Phase 5: Running unit tests..."

if docker run --rm \
    -v "$SCRIPT_DIR/backend:/src:ro" \
    golang:1.22-alpine sh -c "
        cp -r /src /app && cd /app && go mod tidy &&
        go test -v -count=1 -timeout=120s ./pkg/... ./internal/services/ ./internal/handlers/ ./internal/middleware/ 2>&1
    " ; then
    log_pass "Backend unit tests passed"
else
    log_fail "Backend unit tests failed"
fi

# ============================================================
# Phase 6: Frontend tests
# ============================================================
log_info "Phase 6: Running frontend tests..."

if docker run --rm \
    -v "$SCRIPT_DIR/frontend:/src:ro" \
    node:20-alpine sh -c "
        cp -r /src /app && cd /app && npm install --legacy-peer-deps 2>&1 | tail -1 &&
        npx vitest run 2>&1
    " ; then
    log_pass "Frontend tests passed"
else
    log_fail "Frontend tests failed"
fi

# ============================================================
# Phase 7: Cleanup
# ============================================================
log_info "Phase 7: Stopping and cleaning up Docker containers..."
docker compose down -v --remove-orphans

# ============================================================
# Summary
# ============================================================
echo ""
if [ "$FAILED" -eq 0 ]; then
    echo -e "${GREEN}========================================${NC}"
    echo -e "${GREEN}  ALL TESTS PASSED${NC}"
    echo -e "${GREEN}========================================${NC}"
    exit 0
else
    echo -e "${RED}========================================${NC}"
    echo -e "${RED}  SOME TESTS FAILED${NC}"
    echo -e "${RED}========================================${NC}"
    exit 1
fi

#!/usr/bin/env pwsh
$ErrorActionPreference = "Continue"
$scriptDir = Split-Path -Parent $MyInvocation.MyCommand.Path
Set-Location $scriptDir

$failed = 0
function Log-Pass($msg) { Write-Host "[PASS] $msg" -ForegroundColor Green }
function Log-Fail($msg) { Write-Host "[FAIL] $msg" -ForegroundColor Red; $script:failed = 1 }
function Log-Info($msg) { Write-Host "[INFO] $msg" -ForegroundColor Yellow }

# Port configuration (must match docker-compose.yml)
$API_PORT = 8081
$UI_PORT = 3001

Log-Info "Phase 1: Building and starting Docker containers..."
docker compose down -v --remove-orphans 2>$null
$buildResult = docker compose build --no-cache 2>&1
if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) { Log-Pass "Docker build succeeded" }
else { Log-Fail "Docker build failed"; Write-Host $buildResult; exit 1 }

docker compose up -d
Log-Info "Waiting for services to become healthy..."
$healthy = $false
for ($i = 1; $i -le 30; $i++) {
    try {
        $resp = Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "http://localhost:${API_PORT}/api/health" -TimeoutSec 3 -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
        if ($resp.status -eq "ok") { $healthy = $true; break }
    } catch {}
    Start-Sleep -Seconds 2
}
if ($healthy) { Log-Pass "All services are healthy" }
else { Log-Fail "Services not healthy"; docker compose logs; docker compose down -v; exit 1 }

Log-Info "Phase 2: Verifying endpoints..."
try { $h = Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "http://localhost:${API_PORT}/api/health"; if ($h.status -eq "ok") { Log-Pass "Backend health OK" } } catch { Log-Fail "Backend health failed" }
try { $f = Invoke-WebRequest -Uri "http://localhost:${UI_PORT}/"; if ($f.Content -match "WLPR Portal") { Log-Pass "Frontend OK" } } catch { Log-Fail "Frontend failed" }
try { $c = Invoke-WebRequest -Uri "http://localhost:${UI_PORT}/config.js"; if ($c.Content -match "__WLPR_CONFIG__") { Log-Pass "config.js OK" } } catch { Log-Fail "config.js failed" }

Log-Info "Phase 3: Registration and API smoke tests..."
try {
    $reg = Invoke-RestMethod -Method Post -Uri "http://localhost:${API_PORT}/api/auth/register" -ContentType "application/json" -Body '{"username":"testadmin","email":"ta@test.local","password":"TestAdmin@2024!","display_name":"Test Admin","role":"system_admin"}'
    if ($reg.message) { Log-Pass "First user registered (auto-admin)" } else { Log-Fail "Registration failed" }
} catch { Log-Fail "Registration error: $_" }

try {
    $login = Invoke-RestMethod -Method Post -Uri "http://localhost:${API_PORT}/api/auth/login" -ContentType "application/json" -Body '{"username":"testadmin","password":"TestAdmin@2024!"}'
    if ($login.token) {
        Log-Pass "Admin login succeeded"
        $headers = @{ "Authorization" = "Bearer $($login.token)" }
        $me = Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "http://localhost:${API_PORT}/api/auth/me" -Headers $headers
        if ($me.user_id) { Log-Pass "GET /api/auth/me OK" }

        # Register second user with learner role
        $reg2 = Invoke-RestMethod -Method Post -Uri "http://localhost:${API_PORT}/api/auth/register" -ContentType "application/json" -Body '{"username":"testlearner","email":"tl@test.local","password":"Learner@2024!","display_name":"Test Learner","role":"learner"}'
        if ($reg2.message -match "learner") { Log-Pass "Second user registered with learner role" }

        # RBAC test
        $ll = Invoke-RestMethod -Method Post -Uri "http://localhost:${API_PORT}/api/auth/login" -ContentType "application/json" -Body '{"username":"testlearner","password":"Learner@2024!"}'
        $lh = @{ "Authorization" = "Bearer $($ll.token)" }
        try { Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "http://localhost:${API_PORT}/api/procurement/settlements" -Headers $lh; Log-Fail "Learner accessed settlements" }
        catch { if ($_.Exception.Response.StatusCode.value__ -eq 404) { Log-Pass "RBAC: Learner gets 404 (feature invisible)" } else { Log-Fail "RBAC: Got $($_.Exception.Response.StatusCode.value__)" } }
    }
} catch { Log-Fail "Login/API test failed: $_" }

Log-Info "Phase 4: Integration tests..."
$net = (docker network ls --format '{{.Name}}' | Select-String 'repo' | Select-Object -First 1).ToString().Trim()
docker run --rm --network=$net -e "TEST_DATABASE_URL=postgres://wlpr:wlpr_secret@db:5432/wlpr_portal?sslmode=disable" -v "${scriptDir}/backend:/src:ro" -v "${scriptDir}/config/config.js:/config/config.js:ro" golang:1.22-alpine sh -c "cp -r /src /app && cd /app && go mod tidy && go test -v -tags=integration -count=1 -timeout=120s ./internal/repository/ 2>&1"
if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) { Log-Pass "Integration tests passed" } else { Log-Fail "Integration tests failed" }

Log-Info "Phase 5: Unit tests..."
docker run --rm -v "${scriptDir}/backend:/src:ro" golang:1.22-alpine sh -c "cp -r /src /app && cd /app && go mod tidy && go test -v -count=1 -timeout=120s ./pkg/... ./internal/services/ ./internal/handlers/ ./internal/middleware/ 2>&1"
if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) { Log-Pass "Unit tests passed" } else { Log-Fail "Unit tests failed" }

Log-Info "Phase 6: Frontend tests..."
docker run --rm -v "${scriptDir}/frontend:/src:ro" node:20-alpine sh -c "cp -r /src /app && cd /app && npm install --legacy-peer-deps 2>&1 | tail -1 && npx vitest run 2>&1"
if ($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0) { Log-Pass "Frontend tests passed" } else { Log-Fail "Frontend tests failed" }

Log-Info "Phase 7: Cleanup..."
docker compose down -v --remove-orphans

if ($failed -eq 0) { Write-Host "`n  ALL TESTS PASSED" -ForegroundColor Green; exit 0 }
else { Write-Host "`n  SOME TESTS FAILED" -ForegroundColor Red; exit 1 }

{"file_path": "/Users/hana/eglepoint/w2t72/repo/run_test.sh", "content": "#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
cd "$SCRIPT_DIR"

RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
NC='\033[0m'

FAILED=0

log_pass() { echo -e "${GREEN}[PASS]${NC} $1"; }
log_fail() { echo -e "${RED}[FAIL]${NC} $1"; FAILED=1; }
log_info() { echo -e "${YELLOW}[INFO]${NC} $1"; }

# ============================================================
# Phase 1: Backend unit + API tests (no DB required)
# ============================================================
log_info "Phase 1: Running backend unit and API tests..."

if go test -v -count=1 -timeout=120s ./tests/unit/backend/... ./tests/api/backend/... ; then
    log_pass "Backend unit and API tests passed"
else
    log_fail "Backend unit and API tests failed"
fi

# ============================================================
# Phase 2: Integration tests (requires TEST_DATABASE_URL)
# ============================================================
log_info "Phase 2: Running integration tests..."

if [ -z "${TEST_DATABASE_URL:-}" ]; then
    log_info "TEST_DATABASE_URL not set — skipping integration tests"
else
    if go test -v -tags=integration -count=1 -timeout=120s ./tests/e2e/backend/... ; then
        log_pass "Integration tests passed"
    else
        log_fail "Integration tests failed"
    fi
fi

# ============================================================
# Phase 3: Frontend tests
# ============================================================
log_info "Phase 3: Running frontend tests..."

if (cd frontend && npm run test) ; then
    log_pass "Frontend tests passed"
else
    log_fail "Frontend tests failed"
fi

# ============================================================
# Summary
# ============================================================
echo ""
if [ "$FAILED" -eq 0 ]; then
    echo -e "${GREEN}========================================${NC}"
    echo -e "${GREEN}  ALL TESTS PASSED${NC}"
    echo -e "${GREEN}========================================${NC}"
    exit 0
else
    echo -e "${RED}========================================${NC}"
    echo -e "${RED}  SOME TESTS FAILED${NC}"
    echo -e "${RED}========================================${NC}"
    exit 1
fi
"}

{"file_path": "/Users/hana/eglepoint/w2t72/repo/run_test.sh", "content": "#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
cd "$SCRIPT_DIR"

RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
NC='\033[0m'

FAILED=0

log_pass() { echo -e "${GREEN}[PASS]${NC} $1"; }
log_fail() { echo -e "${RED}[FAIL]${NC} $1"; FAILED=1; }
log_info() { echo -e "${YELLOW}[INFO]${NC} $1"; }

# Port configuration (must match docker-compose.yml)
API_PORT=8081
UI_PORT=3001

# ============================================================
# Phase 1: Docker build and startup
# ============================================================
log_info "Phase 1: Building and starting Docker containers..."

docker compose down -v --remove-orphans 2>/dev/null || true

if docker compose build --no-cache 2>&1; then
    log_pass "Docker build succeeded"
else
    log_fail "Docker build failed"
    exit 1
fi

docker compose up -d

# Wait for backend to be healthy
log_info "Waiting for services to become healthy..."
RETRIES=30
HEALTHY=0
for i in $(seq 1 $RETRIES); do
    if curl -sf http://localhost:${API_PORT}/api/health >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        HEALTHY=1
        break
    fi
    sleep 2
done

if [ "$HEALTHY" -eq 1 ]; then
    log_pass "All services are healthy"
else
    log_fail "Services did not become healthy in time"
    docker compose logs
    docker compose down -v
    exit 1
fi

# ============================================================
# Phase 2: Health check endpoints
# ============================================================
log_info "Phase 2: Verifying endpoints..."

if curl -sf http://localhost:${API_PORT}/api/health | grep -q '"status":"ok"'; then
    log_pass "Backend health check returned OK"
else
    log_fail "Backend health check failed"
fi

if curl -sf http://localhost:${UI_PORT}/ | grep -q 'WLPR Portal'; then
    log_pass "Frontend serves HTML with app title"
else
    log_fail "Frontend did not return expected HTML"
fi

if curl -sf http://localhost:${UI_PORT}/config.js | grep -q '__WLPR_CONFIG__'; then
    log_pass "Runtime config.js is served correctly"
else
    log_fail "Runtime config.js not found or invalid"
fi

# ============================================================
# Phase 3: Registration & API smoke tests
# ============================================================
log_info "Phase 3: Registration and API smoke tests..."

# Register admin user (role selected at registration)
REG_RESP=$(curl -sf -X POST http://localhost:${API_PORT}/api/auth/register \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '{"username":"testadmin","email":"testadmin@test.local","password":"TestAdmin@2024!","display_name":"Test Administrator","role":"system_admin"}')

if echo "$REG_RESP" | grep -q 'system_admin\|system admin'; then
    log_pass "Admin user registered with system_admin role"
else
    log_fail "Admin registration failed: $REG_RESP"
fi

# Login as the registered admin
LOGIN_RESP=$(curl -sf -X POST http://localhost:${API_PORT}/api/auth/login \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '{"username":"testadmin","password":"TestAdmin@2024!"}')

if echo "$LOGIN_RESP" | grep -q '"token"'; then
    log_pass "Admin login succeeded"
    TOKEN=$(echo "$LOGIN_RESP" | grep -o '"token":"[^"]*"' | head -1 | cut -d'"' -f4)
else
    log_fail "Admin login failed: $LOGIN_RESP"
    TOKEN=""
fi

if [ -n "$TOKEN" ]; then
    # Test authenticated endpoint
    if curl -sf -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" http://localhost:${API_PORT}/api/auth/me | grep -q '"user_id"'; then
        log_pass "GET /api/auth/me returns user data"
    else
        log_fail "GET /api/auth/me failed"
    fi

    # Test search (empty results expected — no seeded content)
    if curl -sf -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" "http://localhost:${API_PORT}/api/search?q=test" | grep -q '"results"'; then
        log_pass "GET /api/search returns valid response"
    else
        log_fail "GET /api/search failed"
    fi

    # Test learning paths (empty expected — no seeded data)
    PATHS_RESP=$(curl -sf -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" http://localhost:${API_PORT}/api/learning/paths)
    if [ "$PATHS_RESP" = "null" ] || echo "$PATHS_RESP" | grep -q '\[\]'; then
        log_pass "GET /api/learning/paths returns empty (no seeded data)"
    else
        log_pass "GET /api/learning/paths returns valid response"
    fi

    # Register a second user with learner role
    REG2_RESP=$(curl -sf -X POST http://localhost:${API_PORT}/api/auth/register \
        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
        -d '{"username":"testlearner","email":"learner@test.local","password":"Learner@2024!","display_name":"Test Learner","role":"learner"}')

    if echo "$REG2_RESP" | grep -q 'learner'; then
        log_pass "Second user registered with learner role"
    else
        log_fail "Second user registration response unexpected: $REG2_RESP"
    fi

    # Login as learner and verify RBAC invisibility (404 not 403)
    LEARNER_RESP=$(curl -sf -X POST http://localhost:${API_PORT}/api/auth/login \
        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
        -d '{"username":"testlearner","password":"Learner@2024!"}')
    LEARNER_TOKEN=$(echo "$LEARNER_RESP" | grep -o '"token":"[^"]*"' | head -1 | cut -d'"' -f4)

    if [ -n "$LEARNER_TOKEN" ]; then
        HTTP_CODE=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
            -H "Authorization: Bearer $LEARNER_TOKEN" \
            http://localhost:${API_PORT}/api/procurement/settlements)
        if [ "$HTTP_CODE" = "404" ]; then
            log_pass "RBAC: Learner gets 404 for /settlements (feature invisible)"
        else
            log_fail "RBAC: Learner got $HTTP_CODE instead of 404 for /settlements"
        fi

        # Learner should be able to access search
        HTTP_CODE2=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
            -H "Authorization: Bearer $LEARNER_TOKEN" \
            "http://localhost:${API_PORT}/api/search?q=test")
        if [ "$HTTP_CODE2" = "200" ]; then
            log_pass "Learner can access /search (200)"
        else
            log_fail "Learner got $HTTP_CODE2 for /search"
        fi
    fi
fi

# ============================================================
# Phase 4: Integration tests against running DB
# ============================================================
log_info "Phase 4: Running integration tests against containerized DB..."

NETWORK_NAME=$(docker network ls --format '{{.Name}}' | grep repo | head -1)
if [ -z "$NETWORK_NAME" ]; then
    NETWORK_NAME="repo_default"
fi

if docker run --rm --network="$NETWORK_NAME" \
    -e TEST_DATABASE_URL="postgres://wlpr:wlpr_secret@db:5432/wlpr_portal?sslmode=disable" \
    -v "$SCRIPT_DIR:/src:ro" \
    golang:1.22-alpine sh -c "
        cp -r /src /app && cd /app &&
        go work sync &&
        go test -v -tags=integration -count=1 -timeout=120s ./tests/e2e/backend/... 2>&1
    " ; then
    log_pass "Integration tests passed"
else
    log_fail "Integration tests failed"
fi

# ============================================================
# Phase 5: Unit tests (no DB required)
# ============================================================
log_info "Phase 5: Running unit tests..."

if docker run --rm \
    -v "$SCRIPT_DIR:/src:ro" \
    golang:1.22-alpine sh -c "
        cp -r /src /app && cd /app &&
        go work sync &&
        go test -v -count=1 -timeout=120s ./tests/unit/backend/... ./tests/api/backend/... 2>&1
    " ; then
    log_pass "Backend unit and API tests passed"
else
    log_fail "Backend unit and API tests failed"
fi

# ============================================================
# Phase 6: Frontend tests
# ============================================================
log_info "Phase 6: Running frontend tests..."

if docker run --rm \
    -v "$SCRIPT_DIR/frontend:/src:ro" \
    node:20-alpine sh -c "
        cp -r /src /app && cd /app && npm install --legacy-peer-deps 2>&1 | tail -1 &&
        npx vitest run 2>&1
    " ; then
    log_pass "Frontend tests passed"
else
    log_fail "Frontend tests failed"
fi

# ============================================================
# Phase 7: Cleanup
# ============================================================
log_info "Phase 7: Stopping and cleaning up Docker containers..."
docker compose down -v --remove-orphans

# ============================================================
# Summary
# ============================================================
echo ""
if [ "$FAILED" -eq 0 ]; then
    echo -e "${GREEN}========================================${NC}"
    echo -e "${GREEN}  ALL TESTS PASSED${NC}"
    echo -e "${GREEN}========================================${NC}"
    exit 0
else
    echo -e "${RED}========================================${NC}"
    echo -e "${RED}  SOME TESTS FAILED${NC}"
    echo -e "${RED}========================================${NC}"
    exit 1
fi
"}

The backend is actually healthy now — the issue is `set -euo pipefail` causing the script to abort when `docker compose up -d` exits non-zero during the dependency health check race. The fix is to use `--wait` so Docker itself waits for all health checks before returning:

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	set -euo pipefail
3	
4	SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
5	cd "$SCRIPT_DIR"
6	
7	RED='\033[0;31m'
8	GREEN='\033[0;32m'
9	YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
10	NC='\033[0m'
11	
12	FAILED=0
13	
14	log_pass() { echo -e "${GREEN}[PASS]${NC} $1"; }
15	log_fail() { echo -e "${RED}[FAIL]${NC} $1"; FAILED=1; }
16	log_info() { echo -e "${YELLOW}[INFO]${NC} $1"; }
17	
18	# Port configuration (must match docker-compose.yml)
19	API_PORT=8081
20	UI_PORT=3001
21	
22	# ============================================================
23	# Phase 1: Docker build and startup
24	# ============================================================
25	log_info "Phase 1: Building and starting Docker containers..."
26	
27	docker compose down -v --remove-orphans 2>/dev/null || true
28	
29	if docker compose build --no-cache 2>&1; then
30	    log_pass "Docker build succeeded"
31	else
32	    log_fail "Docker build failed"
33	    exit 1
34	fi
35	
36	docker compose up -d
37	
38	# Wait for backend to be healthy
39	log_info "Waiting for services to become healthy..."
40	RETRIES=30
41	HEALTHY=0
42	for i in $(seq 1 $RETRIES); do
43	    if curl -sf http://localhost:${API_PORT}/api/health >/dev/null 2>&1; then
44	        HEALTHY=1
45	        break
46	    fi
47	    sleep 2
48	done
49	
50	if [ "$HEALTHY" -eq 1 ]; then
51	    log_pass "All services are healthy"
52	else
53	    log_fail "Services did not become healthy in time"
54	    docker compose logs
55	    docker compose down -v
56	    exit 1
57	fi
58	
59	# ============================================================
60	# Phase 2: Health check endpoints

The root cause: `set -euo pipefail` made the script exit the moment `docker compose up -d` returned non-zero (which it did because the frontend's `depends_on: backend: condition: service_healthy` failed the race at 10.6s). Now `--wait --wait-timeout 120` tells Docker Compose to block until all health checks pass (up to 120s) before returning, so the script only continues once everything is actually healthy.

content.text:
The container is actually healthy now — the `localhost` → `127.0.0.1` fix you already rebuilt resolved it. Here's the full picture:

**What was wrong:** Inside the nginx alpine container, `wget http://localhost/` failed with "Connection refused" even though nginx was listening on `0.0.0.0:80`. Alpine Linux's minimal libc (`musl`) sometimes fails to resolve `localhost` via `/etc/hosts` the same way glibc does, so `localhost` didn't route to `127.0.0.1`.

**What fixed it:** Changing the Dockerfile healthcheck to `http://127.0.0.1/` bypasses DNS entirely and hits the loopback address directly.

**Current state:** All healthy —
- Container: `Status: healthy`, `FailingStreak: 0`
- Nginx: serving 200s on every 10s check
- Host access: `curl http://localhost:3001/` returns the app HTML
- All built assets present in `/usr/share/nginx/html/`

The frontend is working. You can run `./run_test.sh` and it should get past Phase 1 cleanly.

The `db` and `backend` services have `healthcheck` defined in docker-compose.yml, so `--wait` tracks them and shows `Healthy`. The `frontend` only has its healthcheck in the Dockerfile — Docker Compose's `--wait` doesn't pick that up for status reporting. Fix: add the healthcheck to docker-compose.yml too.

Now `docker compose up --wait` will explicitly track the frontend's health and show `Healthy` alongside db and backend once it passes. The healthcheck matches what's in the Dockerfile (`wget http://127.0.0.1/`).

The healthcheck lines (`127.0.0.1 ... 200 ... Wget`) are fine — that's the health check passing every 10s. The binary garbage is the actual issue:

Those `\x16\x03\x01...` lines are **TLS ClientHello handshakes** — something is sending HTTPS connections to nginx's HTTP-only port 80. Nginx can't speak TLS so it returns `400`. The source IP `172.217.168.145` is a Google-owned IP hitting your exposed port 3001.

Two separate problems:

**1. Nginx is exposed to the internet on port 3001** — port scanners/bots are hitting it. For a dev/test setup, bind to localhost only:

Wait — the `map` directive can't live inside a `server` block, only in `http` context. Let me revert that change since the localhost binding already kills the root cause:

content.text: The file /Users/hana/eglepoint/w2t72/repo/frontend/nginx.conf has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

The localhost binding change in `docker-compose.yml` is the real fix — by changing all ports to `127.0.0.1:PORT:PORT`, Docker only binds to the loopback interface instead of `0.0.0.0`. That means:

- External internet traffic (the Google IP scanner) can no longer reach ports 3001, 8081, or 5433
- The TLS probe spam disappears from nginx logs entirely
- Everything still works from your Mac (`localhost:3001`, `localhost:8081`)

The backend and its healthcheck work fine — `curl` returns `{"status":"ok"}`. The problem is `docker compose up --wait` gives up too early on the `depends_on: condition: service_healthy` before the backend's `start_period: 15s` completes.

The fix: go back to manual health polling instead of `--wait`, but handle the exit code so `set -e` doesn't kill the script.

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	set -euo pipefail
3	
4	SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
5	cd "$SCRIPT_DIR"
6	
7	RED='\033[0;31m'
8	GREEN='\033[0;32m'
9	YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
10	NC='\033[0m'
11	
12	FAILED=0
13	
14	log_pass() { echo -e "${GREEN}[PASS]${NC} $1"; }
15	log_fail() { echo -e "${RED}[FAIL]${NC} $1"; FAILED=1; }
16	log_info() { echo -e "${YELLOW}[INFO]${NC} $1"; }
17	
18	# Port configuration (must match docker-compose.yml)
19	API_PORT=8081
20	UI_PORT=3001
21	
22	# ============================================================
23	# Phase 1: Docker build and startup
24	# ============================================================
25	log_info "Phase 1: Building and starting Docker containers..."
26	
27	docker compose down -v --remove-orphans 2>/dev/null || true
28	
29	if docker compose build --no-cache --progress=plain 2>&1; then
30	    log_pass "Docker build succeeded"
31	else
32	    log_fail "Docker build failed"
33	    exit 1
34	fi
35	
36	log_info "Waiting for services to become healthy..."
37	if docker compose up -d --wait --wait-timeout 120; then
38	    log_pass "All services are healthy"
39	else
40	    log_fail "Services did not become healthy in time"
41	    docker compose logs
42	    docker compose down -v
43	    exit 1
44	fi
45

Build process finished at Fri Apr 10 04:15:56 UTC 2026

Found the issue. The script's health polling only waits for the **backend** to be healthy, then immediately hits the frontend in Phase 2. But the frontend container just started and isn't serving yet.

The fix: wait for both backend AND frontend before proceeding.

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	set -euo pipefail
3	
4	SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
5	cd "$SCRIPT_DIR"
6	
7	RED='\033[0;31m'
8	GREEN='\033[0;32m'
9	YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
10	NC='\033[0m'
11	
12	FAILED=0
13	
14	log_pass() { echo -e "${GREEN}[PASS]${NC} $1"; }
15	log_fail() { echo -e "${RED}[FAIL]${NC} $1"; FAILED=1; }
16	log_info() { echo -e "${YELLOW}[INFO]${NC} $1"; }
17	
18	# Port configuration (must match docker-compose.yml)
19	API_PORT=8081
20	UI_PORT=3001
21	
22	# ============================================================
23	# Phase 1: Docker build and startup
24	# ============================================================
25	log_info "Phase 1: Building and starting Docker containers..."
26	
27	docker compose down -v --remove-orphans 2>/dev/null || true
28	
29	if docker compose build --no-cache --progress=plain 2>&1; then
30	    log_pass "Docker build succeeded"
31	else
32	    log_fail "Docker build failed"
33	    exit 1
34	fi
35	
36	# Start containers (may exit non-zero due to health check race; we poll manually)
37	docker compose up -d || true
38	
39	# Wait for backend to be healthy
40	log_info "Waiting for services to become healthy..."
41	RETRIES=30
42	HEALTHY=0
43	for i in $(seq 1 $RETRIES); do
44	    if curl -sf http://localhost:${API_PORT}/api/health >/dev/null 2>&1; then
45	        HEALTHY=1
46	        break
47	    fi
48	    sleep 2
49	done
50	
51	if [ "$HEALTHY" -eq 1 ]; then
52	    log_pass "All services are healthy"
53	else
54	    log_fail "Services did not become healthy in time"
55	    docker compose logs
56	    docker compose down -v
57	    exit 1
58	fi
59	
60	# ============================================================
61	# Phase 2: Health check endpoints
62	# ============================================================
63	log_info "Phase 2: Verifying endpoints..."
64	
65	if curl -sf http://localhost:${API_PORT}/api/health | grep -q '"status":"ok"'; then
66	    log_pass "Backend health check returned OK"
67	else
68	    log_fail "Backend health check failed"
69	fi
70	
71	if curl -sf http://localhost:${UI_PORT}/ | grep -q 'WLPR Portal'; then
72	    log_pass "Frontend serves HTML with app title"
73	else
74	    log_fail "Frontend did not return expected HTML"
75	fi
76	
77	if curl -sf http://localhost:${UI_PORT}/config.js | grep -q '__WLPR_CONFIG__'; then
78	    log_pass "Runtime config.js is served correctly"
79	else
80	    log_fail "Runtime config.js not found or invalid"
81	fi
82	
83	# ============================================================
84	# Phase 3: Registration & API smoke tests
85	# ============================================================
86	log_info "Phase 3: Registration and API smoke tests..."
87	
88	# Register admin user (role selected at registration)
89	REG_RESP=$(curl -sf -X POST http://localhost:${API_PORT}/api/auth/register \
90	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
91	    -d '{"username":"testadmin","email":"testadmin@test.local","password":"TestAdmin@2024!","display_name":"Test Administrator","role":"system_admin"}')
92	
93	if echo "$REG_RESP" | grep -q 'system_admin\|system admin'; then
94	    log_pass "Admin user registered with system_admin role"
95	else
96	    log_fail "Admin registration failed: $REG_RESP"
97	fi
98	
99	# Login as the registered admin
100	LOGIN_RESP=$(curl -sf -X POST http://localhost:${API_PORT}/api/auth/login \
101	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
102	    -d '{"username":"testadmin","password":"TestAdmin@2024!"}')
103	
104	if echo "$LOGIN_RESP" | grep -q '"token"'; then
105	    log_pass "Admin login succeeded"
106	    TOKEN=$(echo "$LOGIN_RESP" | grep -o '"token":"[^"]*"' | head -1 | cut -d'"' -f4)
107	else
108	    log_fail "Admin login failed: $LOGIN_RESP"
109	    TOKEN=""
110	fi
111	
112	if [ -n "$TOKEN" ]; then
113	    # Test authenticated endpoint
114	    if curl -sf -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" http://localhost:${API_PORT}/api/auth/me | grep -q '"user_id"'; then
115	        log_pass "GET /api/auth/me returns user data"
116	    else
117	        log_fail "GET /api/auth/me failed"
118	    fi
119	
120	    # Test search (empty results expected — no seeded content)
121	    if curl -sf -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" "http://localhost:${API_PORT}/api/search?q=test" | grep -q '"results"'; then
122	        log_pass "GET /api/search returns valid response"
123	    else
124	        log_fail "GET /api/search failed"
125	    fi
126	
127	    # Test learning paths (empty expected — no seeded data)
128	    PATHS_RESP=$(curl -sf -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" http://localhost:${API_PORT}/api/learning/paths)
129	    if [ "$PATHS_RESP" = "null" ] || echo "$PATHS_RESP" | grep -q '\[\]'; then
130	        log_pass "GET /api/learning/paths returns empty (no seeded data)"
131	    else
132	        log_pass "GET /api/learning/paths returns valid response"
133	    fi
134	
135	    # Register a second user with learner role
136	    REG2_RESP=$(curl -sf -X POST http://localhost:${API_PORT}/api/auth/register \
137	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
138	        -d '{"username":"testlearner","email":"learner@test.local","password":"Learner@2024!","display_name":"Test Learner","role":"learner"}')
139	
140	    if echo "$REG2_RESP" | grep -q 'learner'; then
141	        log_pass "Second user registered with learner role"
142	    else
143	        log_fail "Second user registration response unexpected: $REG2_RESP"
144	    fi
145	
146	    # Login as learner and verify RBAC invisibility (404 not 403)
147	    LEARNER_RESP=$(curl -sf -X POST http://localhost:${API_PORT}/api/auth/login \
148	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
149	        -d '{"username":"testlearner","password":"Learner@2024!"}')
150	    LEARNER_TOKEN=$(echo "$LEARNER_RESP" | grep -o '"token":"[^"]*"' | head -1 | cut -d'"' -f4)
151	
152	    if [ -n "$LEARNER_TOKEN" ]; then
153	        HTTP_CODE=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
154	            -H "Authorization: Bearer $LEARNER_TOKEN" \
155	            http://localhost:${API_PORT}/api/procurement/settlements)
156	        if [ "$HTTP_CODE" = "404" ]; then
157	            log_pass "RBAC: Learner gets 404 for /settlements (feature invisible)"
158	        else
159	            log_fail "RBAC: Learner got $HTTP_CODE instead of 404 for /settlements"
160	        fi
161	
162	        # Learner should be able to access search
163	        HTTP_CODE2=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
164	            -H "Authorization: Bearer $LEARNER_TOKEN" \
165	            "http://localhost:${API_PORT}/api/search?q=test")
166	        if [ "$HTTP_CODE2" = "200" ]; then
167	            log_pass "Learner can access /search (200)"
168	        else
169	            log_fail "Learner got $HTTP_CODE2 for /search"
170	        fi
171	    fi
172	fi
173	
174	# ============================================================
175	# Phase 4: Integration tests against running DB
176	# ============================================================
177	log_info "Phase 4: Running integration tests against containerized DB..."
178	
179	NETWORK_NAME=$(docker network ls --format '{{.Name}}' | grep repo | head -1)
180	if [ -z "$NETWORK_NAME" ]; then
181	    NETWORK_NAME="repo_default"
182	fi
183	
184	if docker run --rm --network="$NETWORK_NAME" \
185	    -e TEST_DATABASE_URL="postgres://wlpr:wlpr_secret@db:5432/wlpr_portal?sslmode=disable" \
186	    -v "$SCRIPT_DIR:/src:ro" \
187	    golang:1.22-alpine sh -c "
188	        cp -r /src /app && cd /app &&
189	        go work sync &&
190	        go test -v -tags=integration -count=1 -timeout=120s ./tests/e2e/backend/... 2>&1
191	    " ; then
192	    log_pass "Integration tests passed"
193	else
194	    log_fail "Integration tests failed"
195	fi
196	
197	# ============================================================
198	# Phase 5: Unit tests (no DB required)
199	# ============================================================
200	log_info "Phase 5: Running unit tests..."
201	
202	if docker run --rm \
203	    -v "$SCRIPT_DIR:/src:ro" \
204	    golang:1.22-alpine sh -c "
205	        cp -r /src /app && cd /app &&
206	        go work sync &&
207	        go test -v -count=1 -timeout=120s ./tests/unit/backend/... ./tests/api/backend/... 2>&1
208	    " ; then
209	    log_pass "Backend unit and API tests passed"
210	else
211	    log_fail "Backend unit and API tests failed"
212	fi
213	
214	# ============================================================
215	# Phase 6: Frontend tests
216	# ============================================================
217	log_info "Phase 6: Running frontend tests..."
218	
219	if docker run --rm \
220	    -v "$SCRIPT_DIR/frontend:/src:ro" \
221	    node:20-alpine sh -c "
222	        cp -r /src /app && cd /app && npm install --legacy-peer-deps 2>&1 | tail -1 &&
223	        npx vitest run 2>&1
224	    " ; then
225	    log_pass "Frontend tests passed"
226	else
227	    log_fail "Frontend tests failed"
228	fi
229	
230	# ============================================================
231	# Phase 7: Cleanup
232	# ============================================================
233	log_info "Phase 7: Stopping and cleaning up Docker containers..."
234	docker compose down -v --remove-orphans
235	
236	# ============================================================
237	# Summary
238	# ============================================================
239	echo ""
240	if [ "$FAILED" -eq 0 ]; then
241	    echo -e "${GREEN}========================================${NC}"
242	    echo -e "${GREEN}  ALL TESTS PASSED${NC}"
243	    echo -e "${GREEN}========================================${NC}"
244	    exit 0
245	else
246	    echo -e "${RED}========================================${NC}"
247	    echo -e "${RED}  SOME TESTS FAILED${NC}"
248	    echo -e "${RED}========================================${NC}"
249	    exit 1
250	fi
251

Two fixes:

1. **Frontend race condition**: The script now polls the frontend (`curl http://localhost:3001/`) in a retry loop after the backend is healthy, waiting up to 60s for nginx to start serving. Previously it went straight to Phase 2 endpoint checks before the frontend was ready.

2. **`--progress` flag placement**: Moved from `docker compose build --progress=plain` to `docker compose --progress=plain build` (it's a global compose flag, not a build subcommand flag).

The Only Failure 🔴
text
[POST] /api/auth/register  - 426 (29.53µs) ip=172.19.0.1
HTTP 426 = "Upgrade Required" - The backend is rejecting the registration request.

Root Cause
The Echo framework in Go returns 426 when:

Missing Content-Type: application/json header

Using HTTP/1.0 instead of HTTP/1.1+

Missing required request parameters

The test script is likely making a malformed request to /api/auth/register.

The 426 is from the `AppVersionCheck` middleware. Missing `X-App-Version` header is treated as `"0.0.0"` (the H-02 fix), which fails the version gate on POST requests. The curl commands in run_test.sh need the header.

Let me check what min version is configured:
Min version is `1.0.0` (from init.sql). The fix is to add `X-App-Version: 1.0.0` to all curl requests in the test scripts:

20	UI_PORT=3001

Now fix Phase 6 — the `@testing-library/react` import fails because the test files are in `../tests/unit/frontend/` (outside `node_modules`). On the user's machine this works via a symlink, but `cp -r` in Docker doesn't preserve the symlink target. Need to create the symlink inside the container:

content.name: Edit
content.arguments:
{"replace_all": false, "file_path": "/Users/hana/eglepoint/w2t72/repo/run_test.sh", "old_string": "if docker run --rm \
    -v "$SCRIPT_DIR:/src:ro" \
    node:20-alpine sh -c "
        cp -r /src /app && cd /app/frontend && npm install --legacy-peer-deps 2>&1 | tail -1 &&
        npx vitest run 2>&1
    " ; then
    log_pass "Frontend tests passed"
else
    log_fail "Frontend tests failed"
fi", "new_string": "if docker run --rm \
    -v "$SCRIPT_DIR:/src:ro" \
    node:20-alpine sh -c "
        cp -r /src /app && cd /app/frontend && npm install --legacy-peer-deps 2>&1 | tail -1 &&
        ln -sfn /app/frontend/node_modules /app/tests/unit/frontend/node_modules &&
        npx vitest run 2>&1
    " ; then
    log_pass "Frontend tests passed"
else
    log_fail "Frontend tests failed"
fi"}

Three fixes applied:

1. **Go module location**: Moved `tests/backend/go.mod` up to `tests/go.mod` so the module covers `tests/unit/backend/`, `tests/api/backend/`, `tests/e2e/backend/` as subdirectories. Updated `go.work` to `use ./tests`.

## Key export renames (already done in production code):
- `parseCronInterval` → `services.ParseCronInterval`
- `ledgerToCSV` → `services.LedgerToCSV`
- `settlementsToCSV` → `services.SettlementsToCSV`
- `getExportConfig` → `GetExportConfig` (method on *services.ExportSinkService)
- `RecommendationWorker{maxCatPct: 0.40}` → `services.NewRecommendationWorkerWithPct(0.40)`
- `w.applyDiversityControl` → `w.ApplyDiversityControl`
- `w.deduplicateByContentHash` → `w.DeduplicateByContentHash`
- `ConfigService{configs:..., flags:...}` → `services.NewConfigServiceWithFlags(configs, flags)`
- `parse(input)` (in pkg/config) → `config.Parse(input)`
- `toneStrip(s)` (in pkg/pinyin) → `pinyin.ToneStrip(s)`
- `encryptionKey = key` (in pkg/crypto) → `crypto.SetEncryptionKey(key)`
- `encryptionKey = nil` (in pkg/crypto) → `crypto.SetEncryptionKey(nil)`

## Files to process:

### Services (read from backend/internal/services/, write to backend/tests/services/):

1. `config_service_test.go` → `backend/tests/services/config_service_test.go`
   - `package services_test`
   - Import `"wlpr-portal/internal/services"`, `"wlpr-portal/internal/models"`
   - `&ConfigService{configs: make(map[string]models.Config), flags: map[string]models.FeatureFlag{...}}` → `services.NewConfigServiceWithFlags(make(map[string]models.Config), map[string]models.FeatureFlag{...})`
   - Method calls like `svc.IsFlagEnabled(...)` stay the same since svc is `*services.ConfigService`

2. `scheduler_test.go` → `backend/tests/services/scheduler_test.go`
   - `package services_test`
   - Import `"wlpr-portal/internal/services"`, `"time"`
   - `parseCronInterval(...)` → `services.ParseCronInterval(...)`

3. `export_sink_test.go` → `backend/tests/services/export_sink_test.go`
   - `package services_test`
   - Import `"wlpr-portal/internal/services"`, `"wlpr-portal/internal/models"`
   - The local `mockConfigService` helper uses `&ConfigService{configs:..., flags:...}` → replace with `services.NewConfigServiceWithFlags(configMap, make(map[string]models.FeatureFlag))`
   - `ledgerToCSV(entries)` → `services.LedgerToCSV(entries)`
   - `settlementsToCSV(settlements)` → `services.SettlementsToCSV(settlements)`
   - `sink.getExportConfig()` → `sink.GetExportConfig()`

4. `learning_test.go` → `backend/tests/services/learning_test.go`
   - `package services_test`
   - Import `"wlpr-portal/internal/services"`, `"wlpr-portal/internal/models"`, `"math"`
   - `&RecommendationWorker{maxCatPct: 0.40}` → `services.NewRecommendationWorkerWithPct(0.40)`
   - `w.applyDiversityControl(recs)` → `w.ApplyDiversityControl(recs)`
   - The local `validatePathCompletion` function is defined in the test, keep it as-is
   - The local `intPtr` helper stays in the test file

5. `recommendation_dedup_test.go` → `backend/tests/services/recommendation_dedup_test.go`
   - `package services_test`
   - Import `"wlpr-portal/internal/services"`, `"wlpr-portal/internal/models"`
   - `&RecommendationWorker{maxCatPct: 0.40}` → `services.NewRecommendationWorkerWithPct(0.40)`
   - `w.deduplicateByContentHash(recs, hashMap)` → `w.DeduplicateByContentHash(recs, hashMap)`

6. `reconciliation_test.go` → `backend/tests/services/reconciliation_test.go`
   - `package services_test`
   - No production code imported (all logic is locally defined in the test)
   - Just change package name; keep all local functions as-is

7. `taxonomy_review_test.go` → `backend/tests/services/taxonomy_review_test.go`
   - `package services_test`
   - No production code imported; just change package name

8. `taxonomy_test.go` → `backend/tests/services/taxonomy_test.go`
   - `package services_test`
   - No production code imported; just change package name

### pkg (read from backend/pkg/, write to backend/tests/pkg/):

9. `pkg/config/config_test.go` → `backend/tests/pkg/config/config_test.go`
   - `package config_test`

subagents.description: Deep analysis of why frontend Docker container won't start
subagents.subagent_prompt:
The frontend Docker container for a React/Vite app served by nginx is building successfully but failing to become healthy. Here's what we know:

1. Nginx IS running inside the container and listening on 0.0.0.0:80
2. `wget http://127.0.0.1/` from inside the container returns HTML successfully
3. `wget http://localhost/` fails with "Connection refused" — this was the healthcheck bug (now fixed to use 127.0.0.1 in the Dockerfile)
4. Despite nginx running, the container shows "unhealthy" in Docker

Investigate ALL possible causes. Read the following files carefully:
- /Users/hana/eglepoint/w2t72/repo/frontend/Dockerfile
- /Users/hana/eglepoint/w2t72/repo/frontend/nginx.conf (if exists)
- /Users/hana/eglepoint/w2t72/repo/docker-compose.yml
- /Users/hana/eglepoint/w2t72/repo/frontend/vite.config.js (if exists)
- /Users/hana/eglepoint/w2t72/repo/frontend/package.json

Also run these commands and report ALL output:
- `docker inspect wlpr-frontend 2>/dev/null | python3 -m json.tool` (full state, health, mounts, config)
- `docker exec wlpr-frontend nginx -T 2>/dev/null` (full nginx config dump)
- `docker exec wlpr-frontend ls -la /usr/share/nginx/html/ 2>/dev/null` (check built files exist)
- `docker exec wlpr-frontend wget -qO- http://127.0.0.1/ 2>&1 | head -10` (verify HTTP works)
- `docker exec wlpr-frontend wget -qO- http://localhost/ 2>&1` (check localhost resolution)
- `docker exec wlpr-frontend cat /etc/hosts 2>/dev/null`
- `docker compose logs frontend 2>/dev/null | tail -30` (from /Users/hana/eglepoint/w2t72/repo)
- `curl -s http://localhost:3001/ 2>&1 | head -5` (check from host)

Report: what's the exact root cause, what's the current state of the healthcheck in the Dockerfile, and what exactly needs to change to make the container healthy. Be specific about every file change needed.
